skill.name: text-format-organizer
skill.description: A local text formatting organizer for biomedical and academic writing. Use it to clean whitespace, normalize line endings, and format plain text files while preserving Markdown structure.
skill.summary: Cleans whitespace, normalizes line endings, and formats plain text and Word documents for biomedical and academic writing.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 77 static findings across 8 files (961 lines). All 77 findings are false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' pattern matched description strings and documentation text, not actual crypto operations. The 'external_commands' and 'shell backtick' patterns matched markdown code blocks in SKILL.md showing example usage commands. The 'filesystem' and 'network' hits correspond to path navigation examples and a GitHub URL in documentation. The Python scripts perform legitimate local file processing with no network access, code execution, or data exfiltration capabilities.

medium_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm - False Positive in Documentation
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at multiple locations. All hits are in documentation strings, YAML frontmatter, and markdown table headers. No cryptographic operations exist in the skill's code. Example: scripts/init_run.py:22 is a parser description string; scripts/validate_skill.py:12 is a help text string. Dismissed as false positive.

medium_findings.title: Dynamic Import Expression - False Positive
medium_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'from text_formatter import ...' at scripts/init_run.py:10 as a dynamic import(). This is a standard Python static import, not a dynamic import() call. The module text_formatter is a local module in the same package. Dismissed as false positive.

low_findings.title: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 62 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in SKILL.md. All hits are inside markdown fenced code blocks (```bash ... ```) showing example usage commands to the reader. SKILL.md is documentation, not executable code. No backtick execution exists in the actual Python scripts. Dismissed as false positive.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal in Documentation
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'cd ../academic-proofreader' at SKILL.md:88 as path traversal. This is a relative path navigation example inside a markdown code block showing a multi-step workflow. No actual path traversal exists in the Python code. Dismissed as false positive.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in Documentation
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged the GitHub repository URL at SKILL.md:7 as a network indicator. This is a legitimate source reference URL in markdown documentation. The Python scripts make no network calls. Dismissed as false positive.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance - False Positive
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged documentation text at SKILL.md:118 and audit data at text-format-organizer_audit_result_v2.json:260 as 'system reconnaissance'. Both locations contain legitimate documentation text, not reconnaissance commands. Dismissed as false positive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Code Block with Shell Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md contains markdown fenced code blocks with example bash commands (python scripts/init_run.py). These are documentation examples for the user, not executable code within the skill. No risk of command injection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Python Script with File I/O
dangerous_patterns.description: scripts/init_run.py and scripts/text_formatter.py read and write files based on user-provided paths. This is expected behavior for a file processing tool. Paths are CLI arguments, and the tool validates file existence before processing.

content.user_title: Format and Organize Academic Text Documents
content.value_statement: Biomedical and academic writers often struggle with inconsistent whitespace, mixed line endings, and uneven indentation across files from different editors. This skill cleans trailing spaces, normalizes line endings, removes redundant blank lines, and preserves Markdown and Word document structure, making files submission-ready.
actual_capabilities: Removes redundant empty lines while keeping paragraph boundaries intact
Strips trailing whitespace and normalizes line endings to Unix, Windows, or Mac format
Converts tabs to spaces with configurable indentation size
Preserves Markdown lists, code blocks, and table formatting during processing
Reads and writes plain text, Markdown, and Word .docx files
Provides preview mode to see changes before writing the output file
limitations: Does not spell-check, grammar-check, or rewrite document content
Cannot process PDF files or image-based documents
Does not detect document language or apply citation formatting styles
Requires the input file to exist locally on the machine before processing

use_cases.title: Clean a biomedical manuscript before journal submission
use_cases.description: Remove extra blank lines and trailing spaces from a research paper draft and normalize all line endings to Unix format before journal submission.

use_cases.title: Normalize Markdown notes from multiple sources
use_cases.description: Process Markdown files written on different operating systems to ensure consistent indentation, whitespace rules, and line endings across all files.

use_cases.title: Format a Word document for proofreading pipeline
use_cases.description: Clean a .docx draft by removing empty paragraphs and applying consistent font settings before passing it to a downstream proofreading tool.
use_cases.target_user: Clinical research coordinator or publishing assistant

prompt_templates.title: Quick Markdown cleanup
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the text-format-organizer on myfile.md and save the cleaned version as myfile_clean.md.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user with a single Markdown file

prompt_templates.title: Word document format with custom font
prompt_templates.prompt: Process paper.docx with text-format-organizer. Use Unix line endings and set the output font to Arial 11pt. Save the result as paper_clean.docx.
prompt_templates.scenario: User preparing a Word document with specific formatting requirements

prompt_templates.title: Preview before formatting
prompt_templates.prompt: Run text-format-organizer on draft.md in preview mode so I can see what changes will be made before writing the output file.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to review proposed formatting changes before applying them

prompt_templates.title: Format with change statistics
prompt_templates.prompt: Run text-format-organizer on thesis_chapter.md with verbose and stats mode enabled. Show me how many lines were removed and what formatting changes were applied.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants detailed feedback on the formatting changes made

output_examples.input: A Markdown file with trailing spaces on each line and multiple consecutive blank lines between sections.
output_examples.output: The same Markdown file with all trailing spaces removed, consecutive blank lines collapsed to a maximum of two, and all line endings converted to Unix format.

output_examples.input: A .docx file with inconsistent font styles across paragraphs and several empty paragraphs throughout the document.
output_examples.output: A cleaned .docx file with uniform font applied to all paragraphs, empty paragraphs removed, and all original content formatting preserved.

output_examples.input: A text file using tab indentation with a mix of Windows and Mac line endings imported from multiple editors.
output_examples.output: A normalized text file with tabs converted to 4-space indentation and all line endings unified to the Unix format.

best_practices: Always preview changes first by using the preview flag before writing the final output file.
Specify the output file path explicitly to avoid auto-generated filenames overriding existing files.
Use Unix line endings for cross-platform compatibility when submitting to journal or publisher systems.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to edit the original file in place without creating a backup copy first.
Do not process extremely large files over 100 MB as the tool loads the full content into memory.
Do not rely on this skill for semantic editing, grammar correction, or content review tasks.

faq.question: What file formats does the tool support?
faq.answer: The tool supports plain text (.txt), Markdown (.md), and Word (.docx) files.

faq.question: Will the tool change the content of my document?
faq.answer: No. The tool only changes whitespace, line endings, and indentation. It does not modify words, sentences, or document structure.

faq.question: Does it work with formatted tables and code blocks?
faq.answer: Yes. The tool preserves Markdown table formatting and skips transformations inside fenced code blocks to avoid breaking structure.

faq.question: Can I undo the formatting changes?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as you keep the original file. The tool does not modify files in place unless you specify the same path for input and output.

faq.question: How do I install the required dependencies?
faq.answer: Run pip install python-docx to install the dependency needed for Word document support.

faq.question: Does the tool send my document to an external server?
faq.answer: No. All processing happens locally on your machine. The tool does not make any network requests.
